message:
fix(packages): handle authenticated git URLs, strip userinfo from
install paths, and fix local path deduplication in package source
management

Major overhaul of git source parsing and normalization to correctly
handle authenticated HTTPS URLs (e.g., git:https://token@github.com/
user/repo.git@main) that were previously broken because the naive
split-on-@ logic would incorrectly treat the URL userinfo as a git ref.

Git source parsing (split_git_spec_ref):
- New function that correctly distinguishes URL-embedded @ (userinfo)
  from Pi's trailing @ref syntax by computing a "reserved prefix end"
  position that covers the URL authority section
- For HTTPS URLs (scheme://user@host/...), the reserved zone extends
  through the authority up to the first /
- For SCP-like SSH specs (user@host:path), the reserved zone extends
  through the host:path separator
- Local paths use the simple split since they never contain URL authority
- Scans @ positions from right-to-left, skipping any within the
  reserved zone, to find the actual ref delimiter

Git repo normalization (normalize_remote_git_repo):
- New function that strips URL userinfo (tokens, credentials) from the
  normalized repo identity, preventing secrets from leaking into install
  directory names, trust records, or source matching
- Handles standard HTTPS URLs via url::Url::parse, SCP-like SSH specs
  via manual parsing, and falls back to segment-based normalization
- Always strips .git suffix and leading/trailing slashes

Clone source resolution (git_clone_source):
- New function that preserves the full URL (including userinfo) for
  actual git clone operations, while local paths are resolved relative
  to cwd — decoupling "what to clone" from "how to identify"

Package source matching (sources_match_in_dir, normalize_source_in_dir):
- New cwd-aware variants that resolve sources through parse_source()
  before comparison, so equivalent local paths (./foo/../bar vs ./bar)
  and authenticated vs unauthenticated URLs to the same repo correctly
  deduplicate
- update_package_sources() now accepts cwd and uses the new matching
  functions for both Add (dedup check) and Remove (retention filter)

Tests added:
- sources_match: authenticated URLs with different tokens match
- package_identity: authenticated URL strips userinfo
- installed_path_sync: authenticated and plain URLs resolve to same path
- parse_source: HTTPS with userinfo and ref parses correctly
- normalize_source: git HTTPS with userinfo strips to repo key
- update_package_sources: equivalent local paths deduplicate on add and
  match on remove

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
